route_anchor_evidence.snippet: Figure 1. Effect of curcumin on IL-1 levels in APPSw brains. A, Effect of low-dose curcumin on IL-1. Protein levels were measured in TBS-extracted supernatant from Tg1 mice fed low-dose curcumin diet and untreated Tg1 mice. Levels of IL-1 were significantly decreased by 61.8%. B, Effect of high-dose curcumin on IL-1. Protein levels were measured in TBS-extracted supernatant from Tg1 mice fed a high-dose curcumin diet and untreated Tg1 mice.
Animals were fed either chow containing a low dose of curcumin (160 ppm), a high dose of curcumin (5000 ppm), or no drug for 6 months.

route_raw_labels.endpoint_carrier_snippet: Figure 1. Effect of curcumin on IL-1 levels in APPSw brains. A, Effect of low-dose curcumin on IL-1. Protein levels were measured in TBS-extracted supernatant from Tg1 mice fed low-dose curcumin diet and untreated Tg1 mice. Levels of IL-1 were significantly decreased by 61.8%. B, Effect of high-dose curcumin on IL-1. Protein levels were measured in TBS-extracted supernatant from Tg1 mice fed a high-dose curcumin diet and untreated Tg1 mice.
route_raw_labels.formulation_carrier_snippet: Animals were fed either chow containing a low dose of curcumin (160 ppm), a high dose of curcumin (5000 ppm), or no drug for 6 months.
route_raw_labels.notes: The paper discusses the effects of curcumin on inflammation and oxidative damage relative to ibuprofen in a transgenic mouse model for Alzheimer's disease.

source_notes: High doses did not alter the level of insoluble Aβ but were effective in oxidative protein reduction.
